facts.claim: arXiv is the world’s largest and oldest scientific preprint server.
People with disabilities often cannot read papers on arXiv because they are available almost exclusively in PDF format, which has low native accessibility.
Adding HTML as a format will bring arXiv closer to fulfilling the promise of truly open science.
arXiv is a champion of open science.
arXiv presently holds more than 2.4 million articles.
Cutting-edge research in many fields of Physics, Math, and Computer Science is first made available on arXiv.
arXiv has a mandate to continuously improve access to scientific research.
arXiv's long-term mission is to serve the needs of the scientific community through openness, collaboration and scholarship.
Accessibility is inherent to arXiv's mission of championing open science.
Scientists with disabilities recommended adding HTML as a format for papers to make research more accessible on arXiv.
PDF will always be playing catch up with HTML when it comes to accessibility.
arXiv is growing at an ever-increasing rate.
arXiv has made good progress in making its website more accessible according to W3C WAI guidelines.
arXiv will retain PDFs on arXiv as always.
